promo_headline: Agents week peaks — Anthropic, OpenAI, Google all ship something.
whats_news: Anthropic: Claude can now hold a screen for an hour
Sam Altman: a thread on agent UX defaults

digest.title: The middle of agent week, and the labs all show up.
digest.body: Wednesday is when the labs that have been quiet on Monday and Tuesday all post. Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google each ship an agent feature; Sam Altman defends defaults; Linear adds a review surface. The day reads as proof that agent UX is now the next layer the industry argues about, not the model layer.

items.headline: Claude can hold a screen for an hour
items.deck: Anthropic extended Claude computer-use to one-hour autonomous sessions with periodic check-in approvals. This matters because long-running computer-use is the line between demo and dependable, and Anthropic just crossed it.
items.image_alt: Shaka (Shakyamuni), the Historical Buddha, with Two Attendant Bodhisattvas and Sixteen Arhats — Unknown (14th century)

items.headline: gpt-realtime adds long-form voice mode
items.deck: OpenAI added a long-form voice mode that holds context across 20+ minute sessions. This matters because long-form voice unlocks a new product layer for journaling, coaching, and tutoring apps.
items.image_alt: Don Andrés de Andrade y la Cal — Bartolomé Estebán Murillo (ca. 1665–72)

items.headline: Gemini agents land on Workspace
items.deck: Google brought Gemini agents natively into Docs, Sheets, and Slides with per-doc memory. This matters because Workspace is where most knowledge work happens, and per-doc memory beats per-prompt context for that loop.
items.image_alt: "Portrait of Muhammad Ali Baig", Folio from the Shah Jahan Album — Unknown (recto and verso: early 19th century)

items.headline: Sam Altman on agent UX defaults
items.deck: Sam Altman posted a thread defending the new conservative defaults on consumer agent UX. This matters because the defaults shape what feels normal across every other vendor downstream.

items.headline: Linear opens AI weekly review beta
items.deck: Linear added an AI-generated weekly review for teams that summarizes shipped work and open risks. This matters because weekly review is the most-skipped useful ritual, and Linear is making it cheap to keep.

items.headline: Run one boring real chore on Claude computer-use.
items.why: Today's session-length extension is the only reason the chore now feels worth attempting.
items.try: Set a 45-min timer. Choose: rename a folder of files, or fill a spreadsheet from a PDF. Run it. Watch.
items.image_alt: Battle between Christians and Muslims at El Sotillo — Francisco de Zurbarán (ca. 1637–39)

items.headline: Pick which lab default you want as your team default.
items.why: All three labs just changed defaults the same day. Picking now means you have a stable surface for the rest of the week.
items.try: Set a 25-min timer. Open a doc. Write the three defaults that matter to your team. Choose one provider per row.
items.image_alt: "The Concourse of the Birds", Folio 11r from a Mantiq al-Tayr (Language of the Birds) — Habiballah of Sava (ca. 1600)
